author: @agentbertram4769
text:
Robert, I hope that my taking the mickey about your over use of the word 'awesome' on the eco soap videos, did not cause offence.
You are a rarity in this world. A blend of the savant, the theorist and the pragmatic. I absolutely love your videos and I'm so pleased that you are out there, offering help and advice to us less talented folk.
I'm currently building a wind & solar wood drying kiln... wait for it... in Argyll where the sun don't shine.  I plan to use Heat Tubes, PV solar cells, a Wind Turbine and Supercapacitor storage to heat the air and transport it into and around my kiln. For me, it's a project made in heaven, and practically, I need lots of dry wood for my log burning stove.
I was a controls engineer (not a very good one) working for Johnson Control Systems so I view this project as the culmination of my life's work. I'll probably view the project-after-this-one in the same way.  I did, when I self-built my own log home up here in Argyll.  Every project is the best.  My friends call me 'Topper'.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no mate - i don't - I know you mean well and take the humour in the spirit it is meant. You have an awesom project topper - keep me up to date with it if you would - all the best

replies.author: @agentbertram4769
replies.text: I shall indeed sir.  It is good to have an understanding chum out there.

author: @leokimvideo
text: Your lucky to have only banned so few, I have had massive problems with death threats, hate speech and YouTuber Vs YouTuber. I have been on this site since 2007 and to be honest I really feel the bad vibe has built up severely over the last 3 years. My argument with google staff is this, please don't allow channels to create hate speech. I noticed these channels were flying under the banner of freedom of speech. Google / YouTube are very slack when applying the hate speech pull downs. My argument to them was look at the cumulative actions and hurt a person can do when allowed to post comments that are targeting a producer or other users of the site. When I found it near impossible to have major death threat videos removed it had me look more closely at the flagging and reporting system. I can only assume that it's totally understaffed or the robot doing the work is set to let just about anything be said. YouTube never used to be like this, it's a sad review of the new users of this site...todays next generation, people who hide behind a keyboard thinking they command everything around them. It's very sad to witness, I just hope they get medical help.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +leokimvideo It is a sad state of affairs mate - but freedom of speech is enshrined in the US constitution and i believe the legislative view is that it is best countered by good argument rather than legislation. England has a different view as do many countries but that is the way it is - what that means is that it is up to us to police our own sites - which i try to do and have done from the beginning of the channel. The one thing i try to do is never get into argument with a troll as that's what they love - the second i detect a troll I just ban them without argument. Sometimes i let it go on a bit because i have to try and keep a balance of listening and letting people air contrary views as real argument is important to development and i like real argument and disagreement - but there is a way to do it that is both poilte and respectful - that i insist on

author: @MrInnovativeEnergy
text: How can we help you and support you more? Do you need any equipment or people to do some "grunt work" for you? Are you still running a funding campaign we can support you through? Many of us are electrical and mechanical engineers (including me) that would love to help you in any way we can. I think I can speak for most all who have been following your channel the past few years that we all want to see you succeed in a big way.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Tad Johnson To be honest mate - I am good - it's the moral support that counts a lot for me. I can struggle on - but it is easier knowing i have good wishes behind me - If you are in the UK you are welcome anytime to visit the lab

replies.author: @MrInnovativeEnergy
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith Great! Glad to hear everything is squared away then. I may take you up on the offer to come by and say hi. I'm in the U.S. but I do travel abroad for projects from time to time.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Tad Johnson anytime you are here mate - i will put the coffee on - you bring the biscuits lol

replies.author: @MrInnovativeEnergy
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith Man, do I miss the fish and chips! I'm not sure why, but you just can't get a good plate of fish and chips in the states like you can in the UK. On a side note I just got a call from a friend who is off-grid and lost his second set of lead acid batteries (12 of Trojan L-16, 380Ah) in 7 years time. It really got me thinking about your project and what it could mean for lead acid chemistry in the next few years. I am in charge of developing electronics to improve lead acid battery life span, but I must say lead acid is such an old and horrid chemistry IMHO.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Tad Johnson I made some improvements to lead acid - but as everyone wants better than lithium I didn't do anything with it. It's just sitting on a shelf in the lab!!

author: @eeezygoing
text:
Thanks for this video of appreciation. It is important for people to know that they have helped and given you support as well. It is a two way street isn't it? What's important in all of this, is that people need to express what they feel to each other more openly and even be more open in many issues just as you have been. The positive vibes build up and make both parties stronger and more confident for next time they open their mind and say something lol. There should be more of this in society itself. People in general are very frightened to talk openly about many matters. I used to think it was cos they were indifferent and selfish, but later came to the realisation that they are just inexperienced and fearful that their opinion will be ridiculed. So they sadly never tell others what they really think! The saddest thing is that they would find kindred thoughts in other peoples heads and then they would wonder why they didnt give it a go. But alas they are mostly afraid, so they never find out what others think!
Anyway, in closing, ................ 
Thanks for all you do Robert and do please have a good time in the holidays. As you know they will pass soon and then it's back to the forever grind of work so use the time you have to relax a bit and put your feet up.  

Regards Edward

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +eeezygoing I think it is mate - or at least we should try and make it so - have a good one yourself

author: @wresing
text: I want to share an idea with you on a use for your graphene.  Numberphile recently posted a video called Freaky Dot Patterns. I wonder if you can rotate two graphene films to achieve a similar effect. Can you deposit a single layer over a large enough area to do this experiment, maybe under a microscope, maybe with laser light?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +wresing It's a good idea mate - but - I am quite likely not to get to it anytime soon as I have quite a lot on - any chance of you doing the experiment?

replies.author: @wresing
replies.text: I have been considering ways to deposit thin films of protein to achieve a kind of super magnification of their structure. I have a theory the light will be polarized strangely in a way that might allow one to reconstruct the lattice. Do you think one could use soap bubbles to make a layer one molecule thick?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +wresing  Sounds good mate - we used to make bimolecular leaflets in college chem with a dish of water a little soap and a drop on the surface.

author: @nitelite78
text:
Yeh 14 people banned is nothing. It's when numbers start getting into hundreds or thousands of bans that 'might' give reason to question a channel owner's transparency/honesty etc... I say 'might' because some channels may get lots of unwanted swearing and if they don't want it on their channel they are within their rights as channel owner to ban those people if they want.

The thing that I find quite dissapointing is when comments and ratings are disabled entirely. Although even then there are sometimes legitimate reasons for that, for example using youtube just to archive videos or a company that doesn't have time or capacity to monitor comments. It's more those channels that invite discourse on social/philosophical/religious videos but then disable comments/ratings that gives reason to question the content creator's attitude/motives.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +nitelite78 Absolutely agree mate - it's not communication if it's not two way

author: @Broseficus
text:
Happy New Year Dr. Smith! Thank you for sharing all of your knowledge and experiments with us. It is quite a journey and an inspirational one at that. It takes big brass ones to bring your ides to life and then to share it with us. The truth is most, if not all of the comments here are very supportive and that says a lot about who you are and what you are trying to accomplish. Your banning policy fits right in with the message and spirit of this channel, one of hope and progress. I know it must be very difficult to answer all of your comments and emails that people send to you with all of the work you are doing. I'm sure you will get to mine eventually. 
Keep up the great work Dr. We are all counting on you. (no pressure lol )

author: @adrianionescu5721
text:
One needs a healthy, clean environment to function properly, so I absolutely support removing poisonous, just plainly hateful comments, that are not to be mistaken with constructive criticisms which do have value.  I suppose most haters just hate their own inability of creating anything great, and they try to distroy it in everybody else in a debilitating attempt to feel better...

Other than this, it's amazing to find somebody to resonate with, and that is an example of how somebody with a similar code of values as yours is achieving great things. It's really encouraging,  and makes me put all my efforts to achieve a similar quality.

P.S. Looking forward to buy some of your EESD(s) once you have them ready..

author: @MYNICEEV
text: Firstly. Happy New Year my friend I hope you had a good Christmas, Now. Great video. It is US who should thank YOU for all the great information you pass our way. You deliver it in such a way that even a thicko like me can understand. I was crap at most subjects at school. Chenistry being a big yuk. I have learnt an amazing amount from you over the years and look forward to learning more. And yes I am a thicko. 2 CSEs General Maths and General English. Everything chemistry wise I have learnt't from you, So I am saying to you. THANK YOU ROBERT! Your an awesome teacher. I am very grateful. I am looking forward to more.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Raymond Earle thank you mate and best wishes to you too

author: @numbereight886
text: The way I see it, is that few people make videos. They are invariably not paid and/or receive little other recompense. So, when someone is prepared to impart knowledge FOR FREE I can only say sincere thanks. Merry Xmas and a prosperous New Year!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +IAN MANSELL thank you mate - have a good one yourself

author: @idx9681
text: While I very rarely understand precisely how or what you're doing, as soon as I receive a notification you've uploaded a video, I immediately stop to watch, without fail. Yours is one of the most unique and interesting channels I've ever seen, and you inspire me - and surely many others - to explore and tinker and create, and for that I am quite grateful. When the hard time comes, gather yourself, then laugh in its face as hard as you can muster, knowing you have already made a big change; I learned that if the fates or the world conspire against you, it is because that is what it takes to stop you! Keep it up, I look forward to more inspired content. God bless, and cheers from Texas mate.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Ioshua Xonatser Thank you for sharing that mate - to be honest I hope to help people explore and experiment - it is good to hear that to some degree I do - thans a million

replies.text: +GmanBB cheers mate - i hope they help and any questions feel free to ask

author: @E.lectricityNorth
text: All the best to you in 2016 Robert. I am excited every time I see you working closer toward your goals. The saying, "a gentleman and a scholar", I believe, was probably coined to describe people like you. I truly appreciate your wisdom, knowledge and friendly generosity. Take care and have fun!

We all have just a little richer life because of your contributions. Thank-you. All the Best.
